collection_policy.goal: Build a large-source persona catalog for downstream temporal graph construction without storing copyrighted full text.
inclusion_rules: Keep only bibliographic metadata, edition metadata, collection membership, and paraphrased why-it-matters notes.
Prioritize sources that help reconstruct Zhang Ailing's stable literary voice, autobiographical self-framing, and work timeline.
Allow collection-level or catalog-level URLs when a work or essay is verifiably listed there but lacks a clean standalone metadata page.
exclusion_rules: Do not copy or store copyrighted body text, long quotations, or interview transcripts.
Do not treat user reviews as evidence; use catalog pages only for bibliographic confirmation when needed.
Mark all non-public-domain modern materials as metadata-only even if short snippets appear on source pages.

works.title: 传奇
works.why_it_matters: Early fiction collection that anchors Zhang Ailing's 1940s breakout period and groups several core narrative works into one provenance node.

works.title: 沉香屑：第一炉香
works.why_it_matters: Key Hong Kong-set novella for tracking Zhang Ailing's treatment of desire, transaction, colonial atmosphere, and feminine compromise.

works.title: 沉香屑：第二炉香
works.why_it_matters: Pairs with The First Brazier to show how Zhang Ailing revisits seduction, calculation, and social surfaces through linked atmospheric design.

works.title: 倾城之恋 / Love in a Fallen City
works.why_it_matters: One of the strongest anchors for her war-time urban romance mode and for persona prompts around intimacy under historical instability.

works.title: 金锁记 / The Golden Cangue
works.why_it_matters: Canonical evidence for Zhang Ailing's psychological cruelty, kinship entrapment, and metallic-cold symbolic style.

works.title: 红玫瑰与白玫瑰 / Red Rose, White Rose
works.why_it_matters: Central for modeling her recurring split between fantasy and domesticity, and for male-female self-deception patterns in the graph.

works.title: 半生缘 / Half a Lifelong Romance
works.why_it_matters: Long-form relationship tragedy that helps encode Zhang Ailing's slow-burn realism and the endurance of missed timing.

works.title: 赤地之恋 / Naked Earth
works.why_it_matters: Important for the Hong Kong and Cold War period, showing how her narrative voice shifts when political pressure becomes more explicit.

works.title: 秧歌 / The Rice Sprout Song
works.why_it_matters: Documents how Zhang Ailing handles famine, violence, and rural observation without abandoning her interest in ordinary lives.

works.title: 怨女 / The Rouge of the North
works.why_it_matters: Later reworking of The Golden Cangue that is useful for temporal graph edges about revision, self-adaptation, and late style.

works.title: 色，戒 / Lust, Caution
works.why_it_matters: Late novella that sharpens the relation between erotic risk, espionage, memory, and compromise.

works.title: 小团圆
works.why_it_matters: Autobiographical late novel that links personal history, self-myth, and retrospective emotional patterning.

voice_sources.title: 流言 / Written on Water
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Primary voice corpus for Zhang Ailing's essayistic tone, self-positioning, urban observation, and aphoristic turns.

voice_sources.title: 天才梦
voice_sources.year_or_period: 1940s essay; listed in later collections; accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: One of the clearest self-framing pieces for her ambition, self-consciousness, and literary self-myth.

voice_sources.title: 私语
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Useful for intimate register, family memory, and the cool-confessional mode that later persona outputs should emulate.

voice_sources.title: 自己的文章
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Direct evidence for her writing self-awareness and how she narrates her own craft decisions.

voice_sources.title: 论写作
voice_sources.year_or_period: 1940s essay; collected in Zhang Kan and related editions; accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Provides craft-facing evidence for persona reasoning about style, prose control, and aesthetic priorities.

voice_sources.title: 关于《倾城之恋》的老实话
voice_sources.year_or_period: essay on earlier fiction; collection page accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Links a major fictional work to Zhang Ailing's own retrospective framing, which is highly valuable for voice-grounded commentary.

voice_sources.title: 对照记
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Late autobiographical self-curation source that helps model memory, distance, and selective disclosure.

voice_sources.title: 张看
voice_sources.why_it_matters: A high-yield collection for stitching together daily observation, literary commentary, and self-authored framing notes.

voice_sources.title: 《张看》自序
voice_sources.year_or_period: listed in 惘然记 editions; accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Concise self-positioning document for how Zhang Ailing retrospectively explains one of her own prose collections.

voice_sources.title: 《续集》自序
voice_sources.year_or_period: 1988 collection front matter; metadata page accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Important for tracking how she narrates her post-Shanghai writing period and positions dispersed overseas work.

voice_sources.title: 《连环套》《创世纪》前言
voice_sources.year_or_period: listed in 惘然记 editions; accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Useful bridge between work-level bibliographic nodes and self-commentary nodes in the temporal context graph.

voice_sources.title: 谈看书
voice_sources.year_or_period: later-period essay; listed in 惘然记; accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Supports a readerly persona layer: how she talks about reading habits, taste, and literary judgment.

voice_sources.title: 异乡记
voice_sources.year_or_period: written mid-20th century; posthumous edition metadata accessed 2026-05-13
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Adds first-hand observational prose outside the metropolitan salon frame and helps diversify voice features for graph construction.

biography_and_critical.title: Zhang Ailing | Chinese Novelist & Essayist | Britannica
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Reliable high-level timeline anchor for life stages, migrations, and public identity.

biography_and_critical.title: Chang, Eileen (1920-1995) | Encyclopedia.com
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Strengthens family-background, migration, and literary-reception metadata without requiring direct text reuse.

biography_and_critical.title: Da Roza, Re-imagining the site of the feminine: A rediscovery of Zhang Ailing's fictional works
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Good source for encoding gendered reading frames, the city, and Zhang Ailing's cool but not flat emotional register.

biography_and_critical.title: On the Description of Cityscape in Novels Written by Zhang Ailing (Eileen Chang)
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Directly supports graph attributes around city textures, objects, architecture, and sensory detail.

biography_and_critical.title: Photography and Autobiography: Zhang Ailing's Looking at Each Other
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Useful for linking image, memory, self-staging, and autobiography in late-persona graph nodes.

notes: This catalog intentionally separates works from voice-supporting materials because later temporal graph construction will likely use different node and edge templates for fiction, self-commentary, and critical framing.

Several voice entries point to collection-level metadata pages rather than standalone essay pages. This is a deliberate compromise so the repository can preserve provenance while staying within a strict metadata-only policy.
A next pass should shard collection-level voice sources into finer evidence units with fields such as era, voice_traits, relation_to_work_ids, and usable_graph_edge_templates.
This file does not update the registry yet; it is a collection catalog only, scoped to Zhang Ailing as requested.
